description_long: This object consists of three traditional conical hats, commonly known as nón lá, arranged side by side. Each hat features a wide, circular brim that tapers smoothly to a pointed apex, with a gentle, symmetrical slope. The first hat is painted red with a prominent yellow star, reminiscent of the Vietnamese national flag. The second hat is plain, showcasing a natural straw or bamboo texture with subtle horizontal banding. The third hat is decorated with a floral motif, featuring colorful flowers painted around the brim. All hats have a thin, dark edge at the base, likely representing a reinforced rim. The overall appearance suggests lightweight construction, suitable for sun protection in urban or rural environments.
description: A set of three conical nón lá hats: one red with a yellow star, one plain, and one with floral decorations, each with a wide brim and pointed top.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three hats side by side: red with yellow star on the left, plain in the center, and floral-decorated on the right.
description_view_1: Top-down view with the hats stacked vertically: red with yellow star at the top, plain in the middle, and floral at the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear view with the floral-decorated hat on the left, plain in the center, and red with yellow star on the right.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view with the floral hat at the top, plain in the middle, and red with yellow star at the bottom.
